name: iPay88 Malaysia Payment Gateway API
what_it_does: iPay88 Malaysia payment gateway REST API for merchants to accept FPX (Financial Process Exchange) bank transfers, credit/debit cards, e-wallets (Touch 'n Go, Boost, GrabPay), and QR payments across Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Indonesia, and the Philippines through Southeast Asia's established payment gateway. Enables AI agents to manage FPX bank transfer for Malaysia online banking automation, handle credit and debit card acceptance for Malaysia card commerce automation, access Touch n Go eWallet for Malaysia mobile wallet automation, retrieve GrabPay and Boost e-wallet for Malaysia digital wallet automation, manage QR payment for Malaysia contactless commerce automation, handle payment status inquiry for Malaysia order confirmation automation, access refund processing for Malaysia order management automation, retrieve transaction reporting for Malaysia financial reconciliation automation, manage recurring payment for Malaysia subscription automation, and integrate iPay88 with Malaysian e-commerce, enterprise, and government platforms for end-to-end Malaysia payment automation.
use_cases: Accepting FPX bank transfers from Malaysia online banking checkout agents
Processing Touch n Go e-wallet from Malaysia mobile payment agents
Handling Visa/Mastercard payments from Malaysia card commerce agents
Managing multi-method SEA checkout from Malaysian merchant agents
not_for: Pan-SEA at scale (use Xendit or 2C2P for broader multi-country SEA)
International payments outside SEA (use Stripe or Adyen for global)
Real-time settlement (iPay88 settles T+1 to T+3)
best_when: A Malaysian merchant or SEA business wanting AI agents to automate FPX, e-wallet, and card payment acceptance through iPay88's established Malaysia-native payment gateway with 20+ years of SEA market presence.
avoid_when: FPX BANK PORTAL REDIRECT REQUIRED: FPX payments require customer redirect to their Malaysian bank's online banking portal; automated FPX checkout must redirect customer to FPX gateway; automated server-side FPX bank debit without customer redirect creates payment flow violation. BNM REGULATORY COMPLIANCE: iPay88 operates under Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) regulations; automated payment must comply with BNM payment system rules; automated payment without merchant BNM compliance creates regulatory risk. MERCHANT AUTHENTICATION USING SHA1 HASH: iPay88 requires SHA1 hash of payment parameters for authentication; automated request must compute correct hash; automated request without hash creates authentication failure. E-WALLET REDIRECT REQUIRED: Touch n Go, Boost, and GrabPay require customer redirect to wallet app; automated wallet checkout must redirect customer; automated server-side wallet charge creates unauthorized charge.

auth.notes: iPay88 uses Merchant Code and Merchant Key with SHA1 hash for payment authentication. Form-post and REST API with JSON. Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia HQ. Founded 2001 by Chan Kok Long. Products: FPX, card, Touch n Go, Boost, GrabPay, QR, recurring. SDKs: PHP, .NET, Java. BNM-licensed. PCI DSS. Operations: MY, SG, TH, ID, PH. Competes with Xendit and Razer Merchant Services for SEA payments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — merchant account required
pricing.paid_tiers: FPX: MYR 1-2 per transaction; card: 2-3%; e-wallet: 1-2%; no monthly fee basic
pricing.notes: Kuala Lumpur MY. BNM-licensed. Per-transaction fees in MYR. Merchant agreement required.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per merchant code; payment and inquiry APIs throttled; webhooks for payment completion events

known_agent_gotchas: FORM-POST BASED CHECKOUT: iPay88 uses HTML form POST to iPay88 gateway URL (not JSON REST); automated checkout must redirect customer via form POST; automated JSON REST direct charge creates payment method not supported error

SHA1 HASH PARAMETER CONCATENATION: iPay88 hash requires specific parameter concatenation (MerchantKey+MerchantCode+RefNo+Amount+Currency); automated hash must use exact concatenation order; automated wrong order creates hash mismatch authentication failure

AMOUNT FORMAT WITH NO THOUSAND SEPARATOR: iPay88 amount format is decimal with no thousand separator (e.g., '100.00' not '1,000.00'); automated amount formatting must use decimal without comma; automated comma-formatted amount creates amount parsing error

BACK-END POST URL FOR SERVER CONFIRMATION: iPay88 sends server-to-server payment confirmation to BackendPostURL; automated payment system must implement BackendPostURL endpoint; automated frontend-only checkout without backend post URL creates no server-side payment confirmation
REFNO UNIQUENESS PER TRANSACTION: iPay88 requires unique RefNo per payment request; automated retry must generate new RefNo; automated same RefNo creates reference conflict or duplicate transaction
agent_readiness.security_notes: Malaysia/SEA payments. PCI-DSS, BNM-licensed. Merchant Key/SHA1. MY. FPX and card payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Malaysia payments. PCI-DSS. Merchant Key/SHA1. MY. FPX and card data.
